name: Shoko Relay
overview: A custom Plex metadata provider and automation toolset for integrating Plex and AnimeThemes with Shoko Server.

releases.release_notes:
**Notes:**
- Force refreshing the player with `Ctrl-F5` is recommended
- The WebUI installation method is now live

**Changes:**
- Allow batch Theme MP3 generation for deeply nested directories and track if a theme has already been generated for a given AniDB ID
- Added validation checks to single and batch MP3 generation pipelines to detect and reject VFS root folder segments
- Implemented an Audio Only toggle button and `V` hotkey to hide the video layout and stop active rendering (player page)
- Implemented the `R` hotkey to restart the currently active theme from the beginning (player page)
- Parse mapped AnimeThemes that do not correspond to an anime in the local Shoko collection and add them to the video player tree
- Group unmapped anime using physical base name extraction and append them to a unified "Missing from Collection" branch
- Add sorting logic to `player.js` to force the "Missing from Collection" branch to the top of the browser list
- Added `AnimeThemesWebmDownloader` and exposed it as an HTTP POST endpoint for fetching and organizing .webm files into Year/Season directories
- Fixed the "jump to" action sometime failing on the player page
- Enhanced the build workflow to inject release notes into the manifest
- Updated the readme/controller.md to account for the new changes
- Bumped Shoko Abstractions to v6.0.0-alpha.44

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/natyusha/ShokoRelay/compare/v0.13.1...v0.13.2

**Notes:**
- Force refreshing the vfs browser with `Ctrl-F5` is recommended

**Changes:**
- Map active AnimeThemes files directly into the VFS Browser's blueprint database during generation allowing them to be seen
- Natural sort the season folders in the vfs browser and place them after non season folders in the tree
- Prune redundant, multi-line record instantiation blocks inside AnimeThemesMapping and its helper LoadThemeMappings
- Update browser.js index mapping to support metadata link resolving for all extra season folders
- Dynamically append the resolved Plex URL to the Plex Web App button's tooltip on hover
- Implement prefix-based "m{ShokoID}" and "a{AniDBID}" search filtering to target series IDs directly
- Restrict numeric AniDB ID filtering in the VFS Browser to only match when prefixed with "a"
- Force single column dashboard layout at 925px media width and set header logo and title margins to 0
- This ensures that the header text doesn't overflow at 700px window width (for the WebUI iframe)
- Parse CSharpier output in the lint workflow to emit native GitHub inline error annotations
- Generate a rich Markdown report and append it directly to the GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
- Utilize dotnet tool restore to download CSharpier based on the committed .config/dotnet-tools.json manifest
- Refactor MapHelper/VfsBuilder/VfsWatcher with some general performance/style/stability tweaks
- Remove redundant backing fields across the project
- Directly reference modern primary constructor parameters across all refactored service methods
- Evaluate input.value on initial search interaction setup to determine the clear button's visibility on the player/browser pages
- Bump Shoko Abstractions to v6.0.0-alpha.38

**Pull Requests:**
* feat: add plugin page by @revam in https://github.com/natyusha/ShokoRelay/pull/8

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/natyusha/ShokoRelay/compare/v0.13.0...v0.13.1

This is one of the larger releases in a while feature wise but now I can say that all of the extended additions that were planned are complete.

**Notes:**
- Force refreshing the dashboard/browser/player with `Ctrl-F5` is recommended
- The "Collection Posters Root Path" setting no longer exists and was replaced with the "Collection Images Root Path" (there is no migration)

**Changes:**
- Upgraded VFS series pruning logic to resolve directories directly via active managed folders, ensuring empty or deleted series folders and their metadata are recursively cleaned up.
- Simplified Shoko VFS import ignore rules by evaluating the full absolute path of scanned files.
- Added a dynamic Plex Web App link to the authentication panel, automatically resolving the best connection URI from the discovered servers.
- Optimized path mapping translation speed and memory allocations in the Plex client by introducing thread-safe caching and eliminating redundant LINQ allocations.
- Optimized smart collection artwork scans and Shoko import scans to eliminate database-heavy loops by resolving global roots once and utilizing thread-safe caching for ignored folder names.
- Expanded collection generation to support mapping, syncing, and automatic local disk pruning for collection posters, backdrops, clear logos, and square art.
- Refactored watched-state syncing to Plex to support multiple Plex RatingKeys per Shoko ID, ensuring all matching virtual segments of split or grouped episodes are correctly scrobbled.
- Implemented bi-directional watched state syncing and webhook playback progress tracking (Plex to Shoko) to populate the "Continue Watching" section in Shoko's WebUI.
- Introduced an "Include Progress" toggle to the configuration settings and sync menu modal to control progress synchronization, complete with automatic checks to prevent progress updates from overwriting fully-watched states.
- Cleaned up the codebase by migrating inline namespace declarations to modern file-scoped usings, and removing several unused, unreferenced, or redundant helper methods, constants, and config parameters.
- Polished the dashboard, browser, and player layouts with CSS flexbox styling, robust tooltip positioning for disabled states, optimized web font subsets, and a visual progress bar for timed toast notifications.
- Bumped Shoko.Abstractions to v6.0.0-alpha.32.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/natyusha/ShokoRelay/compare/v0.12.7...v0.13.0
